data.description:

Horizon Oil Limited ( (AU:HZN) ) has provided an announcement.

Horizon Oil has reported a change in the interests of director Richard Beament following an adjustment to his equity-based incentives. Beament and his related party continue to hold a substantial stake in the company, including ordinary shares and a growing pool of performance rights that link his remuneration to shareholder returns.

The director received additional deferred short-term incentive rights and long-term performance rights at no cash cost, triggered by an adjustment under the company’s incentive plan rules following the declaration of a 2026 interim unfranked dividend. The move modestly increases Beament’s exposure to Horizon Oil’s future performance, further aligning his interests with those of investors as dividends are paid and incentive plans are recalibrated.

More about Horizon Oil Limited

Horizon Oil Limited is an energy company focused on the exploration and production of oil and gas. The company is listed on the ASX and its shareholder base includes directors and related parties who hold ordinary shares as well as performance-based equity rights aligned with long-term incentive plans.

data.title: Optorun Clarifies Independence and Transactions With Major Affiliated Shareholder
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

OPTORUN CO.,LTD. ( (JP:6235) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Optorun disclosed details of its relationship with Zhejiang Crystal-Optech Co., Ltd., which holds 16.33% of its voting rights and is classified as an other affiliated company listed on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange A-share Main Board. Despite this significant stake and a business relationship involving product sales exceeding ¥1.23 billion in 2025, Optorun emphasized that it operates without managerial constraints from Zhejiang Crystal-Optech and continues to conduct its business activities independently, underscoring its governance autonomy and clarifying related-party transactions for investors.

The company reported that transactions with Zhejiang Crystal-Optech primarily consist of product sales, generating advance receipts and accounts receivable on Optorun’s balance sheet. By detailing these figures and the absence of control influence, Optorun aims to enhance transparency around its shareholder structure and related-party dealings, which can help stakeholders better assess potential conflicts of interest and the impact of this strategic shareholder on its operational and financial profile.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:6235) stock is a Buy with a Yen3300.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on OPTORUN CO.,LTD. stock, see the JP:6235 Stock Forecast page.

More about OPTORUN CO.,LTD.

Optorun Co., Ltd., listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Prime Market, operates in the optical and semiconductor-related equipment industry, supplying specialized products and technologies for advanced manufacturing. The company serves global customers and maintains capital and business relationships with overseas optical component manufacturers as part of its broader corporate group strategy.

An update from Oceania Healthcare ( (AU:OCA) ) is now available.

Oceania Healthcare has scheduled the release of its full-year financial results for the 12 months to 31 March 2026 for the morning of Friday, 22 May 2026. The results presentation will be made available via both the NZX and ASX, as well as on the company’s investor relations website.

The company will also host a webcast at 11:00 a.m. NZST (9:00 a.m. AEST) on 22 May 2026, led by chief executive Suzanne Dvorak and chief financial officer Kathryn Waugh. data.title: Emu NL Raises $1.63m to Fund Major Yataga Valley Drilling Campaign
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Emu NL ( (AU:EMU) ) has shared an announcement.

Emu NL has closed its non-renounceable rights issue, raising approximately $1.63 million through subscriptions for about 65.4 million new shares, each with a free attaching option exercisable at $0.03. Following the issue, the company’s capital structure now comprises 351.5 million fully paid ordinary shares on issue, alongside various partly paid shares and a substantial suite of listed and unlisted options.

Proceeds from the raising will fund a focused 10,000-metre reverse circulation and diamond drilling program at Yataga Valley, aimed at testing the core of the intrusive complex beneath coherent copper anomalies and key geophysical targets. The program is expected to generate high-quality geological data, refine the project model, and potentially reposition Emu NL within the copper exploration space, although a $2.03 million shortfall in the offer remains, with brokers engaged on a best endeavours basis to place it.

More about Emu NL

Emu NL is an Australian-listed resources company focused on mineral exploration. The company is advancing projects including the Yataga Valley prospect, where it is targeting copper and associated sulphide mineralisation through drilling to test the core of the Yataga Intrusive Complex.

Average Trading Volume: 359,390

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: A$10.3M

Greatview Aseptic Packaging Company ( (HK:0468) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Greatview Aseptic Packaging Company Limited has proposed a final ordinary dividend of HKD 0.006 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, subject to shareholder approval on 23 June 2026. The dividend, payable in Hong Kong dollars with an ex-dividend date of 30 June 2026 and payment scheduled for 6 August 2026, signals ongoing capital returns to shareholders and provides income visibility for investors while underscoring the company’s commitment to shareholder remuneration.

The entitlement to the dividend will be determined based on a record date of 7 July 2026, following a book closure period from 3 July to 7 July 2026 administered by Tricor Investor Services Limited as share registrar. The announcement, which also confirms the current composition of the board with a mix of executive, non-executive, and independent non-executive directors, highlights a stable governance structure supporting the company’s dividend policy and investor confidence.

More about Greatview Aseptic Packaging Company

Greatview Aseptic Packaging Company Limited operates in the packaging industry, focusing on aseptic packaging solutions for liquid food and beverage producers. Listed in Hong Kong under stock code 00468, the company serves brand owners that require shelf-stable, hygienic packaging formats and competes in a global market for food-grade packaging materials and related services.

data.title: Recharge Metals ends minor project talks after ASX price query
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Recharge Metals Limited ( (AU:REC) ) just unveiled an update.

Recharge Metals has clarified that recent trading volatility in its shares coincided with early stage, confidential negotiations over a potential project acquisition that it did not consider material. The company has since terminated discussions and will not pursue the project, stating it does not expect the abandoned deal to have a material effect on the price or value of its securities, and confirming full compliance with ASX continuous disclosure rules and board approval of its responses.

REC acknowledged that it had relied on ASX Listing Rule 3.1A to withhold disclosure while talks remained preliminary, but requested a trading halt once the ASX issued a price query and the company could no longer rely on confidentiality. Beyond the now-terminated negotiations, Recharge said it is unaware of any other undisclosed information that could explain the recent price and volume movements in its stock, seeking to reassure investors and regulators about its disclosure practices.

More about Recharge Metals Limited

Recharge Metals Limited is an Australia-based exploration company listed on the ASX under the code REC. The company focuses on identifying and acquiring mineral projects, regularly reviewing potential project opportunities as part of its growth and portfolio strategy.

data.title: Eureka Group accelerates regional expansion with $14m Victorian park acquisitions
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Eureka Group Holdings Limited ( (AU:EGH) ).

Eureka Group Holdings has entered binding agreements to acquire Frenchview Lifestyle Village in Grantville and Paynesville Holiday Park in East Gippsland, adding 199 sites across predominantly land-lease and mixed-use residential communities. The $14.1 million off-market purchases, on initial yields of 7.9% and 7.6% respectively, lift Eureka’s all-age rental portfolio by about 17% and underscore its strategy to grow in demographically attractive regional markets.

Both assets come with development-ready sites and scope for reconfiguration, densification and potential adjoining land at Paynesville, offering embedded growth beyond initial income returns. The deals take Eureka to eleven all-age rental village acquisitions in 15 months and sit alongside a further $90 million pipeline under due diligence or price discovery, reinforcing the group’s expansion drive and signalling continued portfolio scale-up for investors and residents alike.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:EGH) stock is a Buy with a A$0.85 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Eureka Group Holdings Limited stock, see the AU:EGH Stock Forecast page.

More about Eureka Group Holdings Limited

Eureka Group Holdings Limited is an ASX-listed owner and operator of rental communities, with a growing focus on all-age and land-lease villages in regional Australia. The group targets markets benefiting from strong population growth and demand for affordable housing, particularly in coastal and regional areas of Victoria and other states.

data.title: Algorhythm Holdings Earnings Call Shows Risky Growth Pivot
data.description:
Algorhythm Holdings, Inc. ((RIME)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Algorhythm Holdings’ latest earnings call struck a cautiously optimistic tone, pairing eye-catching growth metrics with sobering financial realities. Management showcased surging revenue, strong customer validation and a healthier balance sheet, yet persistent gross losses and a sizable net loss kept the story squarely in “early innings” territory rather than a full financial turnaround.

Explosive Revenue Growth from SemiCab India Acquisition

Sales jumped 1,370% to $4.4 million in 2025, up from just $0.3 million a year earlier, almost entirely due to the May acquisition of SemiCab India (SMCB). The unit delivered the full $4.4 million in only eight months under Algorhythm ownership, underscoring how central the deal has become to the company’s near-term growth story.

Run-Rate Near $10 Million and Ambitious Scaling Targets

Management reported an annualized revenue run rate approaching $10 million by December 2025 and said it was already higher in early 2026. They are targeting a run rate of $15 million to $20 million by the end of 2026, driven by continued expansion in India managed services and the first wave of SaaS revenue from the SemiCab Apex platform.

Fortune 500 Wins and Pilots Converting to Large Contracts

The company highlighted four new Fortune 500 customers in India, five pilot programs that expanded into multimillion-dollar deals and new wins with MTR Foods and Coca-Cola India in the first quarter of 2026. Management stressed that every pilot customer has asked for increased geographic or volume coverage, signaling strong product-market fit and meaningful upsell potential.

Operational Proof Points: Fewer Empty Miles and Higher Throughput

In live operations, the SemiCab platform has cut empty truck miles by more than 70%, a key efficiency gain for shippers and carriers. It also supports up to four times more freight volume without adding headcount compared with traditional brokers, suggesting compelling cost, utilization and emissions benefits if the model can scale.

Apex SaaS: High-Margin, Asset-Light Growth Option

Management is pinning long-term margin expansion on SemiCab Apex, a global SaaS product designed to sit alongside existing transportation management systems. They described Apex as asset-light and easily integrable, with the potential to deliver roughly 90% SaaS-like margins and rapid global scalability, creating a promising high-margin recurring revenue stream.

Liquidity Improving and Liabilities Cut Almost in Half

Algorhythm reported that cash on hand rose from $6.1 million at year-end 2025 to $10.9 million by late March 2026, an increase of about 79%. Over the same period, liabilities were reduced by roughly 50% year over year, improving the company’s leverage profile and giving investors more comfort around near-term liquidity.

Operating Expenses Down, But G&A and Growth Spend Rising

Operating expenses fell nearly 20% to $6.6 million in 2025 from $8.2 million in 2024, helped by the absence of a $3.6 million goodwill impairment booked in the prior year. However, general and administrative costs rose about $2 million in 2025 and are expected to climb as the company funds commercial expansion, partially offsetting efficiency gains.

Negative Gross Margins Highlight Ramp Costs

Despite revenue growth, Algorhythm posted a $1.3 million gross loss in 2025, deeper than the $194,000 loss a year earlier, reflecting negative gross margins. Management attributed this to the managed services model, where contracts to secure truck capacity front-load costs while route density and revenue build more gradually during ramp-up.

Net Loss Still Large Despite Year-Over-Year Improvement

Net loss from continuing operations narrowed to $15.2 million in 2025 from $18.9 million in 2024, a roughly $3.7 million improvement but still a significant drag. Management expects losses to shrink as revenue scales and noncash charges normalize, yet the current deficit remains a major risk factor for shareholders.

Revenue Concentration in India Managed Services

2025 growth depended heavily on the acquired India managed services business, with SemiCab contributing the entire $4.4 million in revenue over eight months. The legacy U.S. SemiCab operation generated only $300,000 in 2024, leaving Algorhythm exposed to concentration and execution risk until Apex SaaS and other geographies contribute meaningfully.

Slow Enterprise Sales Cycles Cloud SaaS Ramp

While Apex is commercially ready, management acknowledged that adoption hinges on lengthy and cautious enterprise procurement processes. Large logistics service providers are moving slowly, with pilots and tests taking time and no immediate wave of signings, making the pace and scale of SaaS revenue a key uncertainty.

Noncash Warrant Charges and Higher G&A Weigh on Results

Algorhythm recorded $6.5 million in noncash warrant charges in 2025, following $8.9 million in 2024, inflating reported losses. Rising G&A spending, driven by growth investments and public company costs, also weighed on profitability, suggesting that near-term margin improvement will be gradual even as operating expenses trend lower.

Restricted Cash and Contingent Funding Add Complexity

Management noted that a portion of its cash balance is restricted in a reserve tied to a prior financing transaction and will be released only as certain securities purchases occur. That structure means part of the liquidity picture depends on future events and timing, adding a layer of complexity to the company’s cash management.

Strategic Shift Marked by Discontinued Consumer Electronics Unit

The sale of the legacy consumer electronics business, now treated as discontinued operations, underscores Algorhythm’s pivot toward logistics technology and managed services. It also signals that reported results are in a transition phase, with investors needing to focus on the emerging freight and SaaS franchise rather than historical hardware revenues.

Guidance: Faster Revenue, Better Margins, Ongoing Investment

Looking ahead, management is guiding to a sharp ramp in the revenue run rate from nearly $10 million in late 2025 to $15 million to $20 million by the end of 2026, fueled by India managed services growth and Apex SaaS traction. They expect gross loss as a share of revenue and net loss from continuing operations to decline over the next year while maintaining liquidity and continuing to invest in growth.

Algorhythm’s call painted the picture of a company gaining commercial momentum but still working through a financially painful transition. Record revenue growth, strong customer expansions and operational proof points are encouraging, yet negative margins, a sizable net loss and sales-cycle risk mean investors must weigh significant execution risk alongside the upside potential.
